matches.recap: Mexico's misery in World Cup opening games was finally ended as they secured a 2-0 win over South Africa at the Estadio Azteca in a match featuring three red cards.
South Korea rallied from 0-1 down to defeat the Czech Republic 2-1 in the second match of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Substitute Cyle Larin scored in the 78th minute and co-host Canada earned a first point in their third World Cup appearance -- and first on home soil -- by rallying for a 1-1 draw against Bosnia-Herzegovina on Friday.
The United States cruised past Paraguay to open its home World Cup campaign with a statement victory in front of a galaxy of Los Angeles stars.
Qatar scored a last-gasp equaliser to secure their first World Cup point by snatching a draw against Switzerland on Saturday.

matches.venue: MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, New Jersey
matches.recap: Vinícius Júnior scored an equalizing goal in the 32nd minute, giving five-time champion Brazil a 1-1 draw against Morocco on Saturday.
John McGinn deflected a shot off an opposing defender and past goalkeeper Johny Placide in the 28th minute, and Scotland defeated Haiti 1-0 in Group C of the World Cup on Saturday.
Nestory Irankunda and Connor Metcalfe scored and Australia spoiled Türkiye's return to the World Cup for the first time in 24 years with a 2-0 victory on Saturday night.
Kai Havertz scored two goals as Germany pulled away from World Cup first-timer Curaçao late in the opening half and piled on after that in a 7-1 win Sunday in a group match.
Japan hit back twice to start their World Cup campaign with an entertaining 2-2 draw against the Netherlands.
A late goal from Amad Diallo earned the Ivory Coast a 1-0 win in and end-to-end opening World Cup clash with Ecuador in Philadelphia.
Yasin Ayari scored twice, while Alexander Isak and Viktor Gyökeres were also on target as Sweden cruised to a dominant 5-1 victory over Tunisia to move atop World Cup Group F.
Cape Verde pulled off the shock of the 2026 World Cup thus far as they held European champions Spain to a battling goalless draw in their Group H opener in Atlanta.

Romelu Lukaku's mere presence sparked an attack that led to Belgium's lone goal only seconds after he entered as a second-half substitute, helping his team earn a 1-1 draw with Egypt on Monday at the World Cup.

matches.venue: Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, Florida
matches.recap: Maxi Araújo rescued a point for Uruguay to deny Saudi Arabia another famous World Cup win as the sides drew 1-1 in Miami.
Ramin Rezaeian and Mohammad Mohebbi scored for Iran, with Eli Just grabbing a brace for New Zealand.

matches.venue: MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, New Jersey
matches.recap: Mbappe scores brace as France make statement in 3-1 win over Senegal
Erling Haaland scored his first World Cup goals with a brace as Norway started their campaign with a 4-1 win over Iraq in Boston.

matches.venue: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City, Missouri
matches.recap: Lionel Messi equalled Miroslav Klose's record of 16 World Cup goals with a hat-trick as Argentina began their title defence with a 3-0 win over Algeria in Kansas City.
Austria won a World Cup game for the first time in 36 years with a 3-1 victory Tuesday over a Jordan team making their debut at the global showpiece.
Cristiano Ronaldo and his Portugal teammates could only manage a 1-1 draw with Congo DR on Wednesday in their opening World Cup match.
Caleb Yirenkyi scored in the fifth minute of second-half stoppage time as Ghana beat Panama 1-0 on Wednesday in the teams' World Cup opener.
Second-half goals from Luis Díaz and Jáminton Campaz gave Colombia a 3-1 victory over debutants Uzbekistan.
A late penalty from Teboho Mokoena secured South Africa a 1-1 draw with the Czechia in Atalanta, which keeps alive both sides' hopes of qualification from Group A.

Substitute Johan Manzambi scored his first World Cup goal on an outstanding volley in the 74th minute, and Switzerland erupted late for a 4-1 victory over Bosnia-Herzegovina on Thursday to take control of its group.
Canada beat 9-men Qatar 6-0 to go top of Group B
A Luis Romo goal saw Mexico beat South Korea 1-0
The U.S. advanced to the knockout round of the World Cup without their injured star forward, beating Australia 2-0 on Friday.
Scotland's spirited second-half rally was not enough to bring any World Cup joy as they suffered a frustrating 1-0 defeat to Morocco in Foxborough.
Brazil moved top of World Cup Group C with a 3-0 win over Haiti
10-man Paraguay eliminate Turkey with 1-0 win
Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two each as Netherlands cruised to a 5-1 World Cup victory over Sweden to go top of Group F.
A brace by substitute Deniz Undav fired Germany to a last-gasp 2-1 win over Ivory Coast to secure their place in the last 32 of the World Cup.

matches.venue: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City, Missouri
matches.recap: Room made 15 saves - the most by any goalkeeper in 90 minutes of a World Cup match since records began in 1966
A dominant Japan produced the biggest margin of victory for an Asian nation in a World Cup match as they beat Tunisia 4-0
Spain got their World Cup campaign back on track with a 4-0 victory over Saudi Arabia in Atlanta.
Alireza Beiranvand made seven saves as Iran and 10-man Belgium played to a 0-0 draw at the World Cup on Sunday.

matches.venue: Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, Florida
matches.recap: After holding Spain to a 0-0 draw, Cape Verde pulled off another impressive result with a draw against Uruguay.
Mohamed Salah scored the go-ahead goal, and Egypt went on to defeat New Zealand 3-1 on Sunday night and secure a first-ever World Cup win.
Lionel Messi set a World Cup record with his 17th and 18th goals, and defending champion Argentina advanced to the knockout stage with a 2-0 victory over Austria on Monday.
Kylian Mbappé scored twice to move into a tie for second in career World Cup goals with 16, and France played through the tournament's first rain delay to beat Iraq 3-0 on Monday.

matches.venue: MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, New Jersey
matches.recap: Norway sensation Erling Haaland scored two stellar goals to lead his team to a fairly routine 3-2 win over Senegal at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts, on Monday.
